content.problemStatement:
Given a list of items, each with a positive integer size, and a set of identical bins, each with a fixed capacity, the goal is to determine the minimum number of bins required to pack all the items, such that no bin's capacity is exceeded.

Can you solve this problem using the following approach


Sort the list of item sizes in non-increasing order. This is done to utilize the "First Fit" strategy efficiently.
Initialize an array of bins, each with a capacity equal to the given bin capacity.
Iterate through the sorted item sizes:

For each item size, try to place it in the first bin where its size fits without exceeding the bin capacity.
If no suitable bin is found, create a new bin and place the item in it.


The number of used bins is the answer.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of items and the capacity of each bin.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, ithi^{th}ith of which denotes AiA_iAi​ the size of the items




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum number of boxes required to pack the items
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and M — the number of items and the capacity of each bin. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, the sizes of the items.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of bins used by the First Fit Decreasing algorithm.

solution.approach: Sort the items in non‑increasing order. Then process each item and place it into the first bin that still has enough remaining capacity. If no existing bin can accommodate the item, open a new bin. This is the classic First Fit Decreasing (FFD) heuristic which runs fast and gives the optimal answer for the given constraints.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) for sorting plus O(N * B) for placement, where B ≤ N. With N ≤ 1000 this easily fits within limits (worst‑case O(N^2)).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the items and the remaining capacities of the bins.
